topics.title: Chronic Pain Phenotypes and Brain Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster focuses on chronic pain disorders, especially fibromyalgia, migraine, and trigeminal neuralgia, with attention to pain perception and related clinical features. It also highlights central sensitization and brain-based phenotypes underlying pain experience and analgesia.

topics.title: Neural Circuits in Substance Use Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related disorders and substance use across alcohol, cannabis, methamphetamine, and opioids, with emphasis on craving, cues, reward, and inhibitory control. It also includes adolescent brain development and white matter/connectivity changes linked to exposure and abstinence.

keywords: lsd reshapes cortical dynamics through peak frequency shifts
topics.title: Psychedelics and Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psychedelics and ketamine alter brain activity, connectivity, and perceptual states. It emphasizes EEG/fMRI-based network analyses and modeling approaches used to characterize acute drug effects and cortical dynamics.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
neurometabolic mechanisms of dynamic musical reward processing
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Music, Reward, and Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music engagement, training, and participation shape neural reward, memory, and sensorimotor systems. It also includes studies on emotional responses, musical skill retention, and structural or functional pathways linking music experience to brain organization.

topics.title: OCD Circuitry and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, related compulsive traits, and frontostriatal/cortico-striatal-thalamo-cortical circuitry. It also emphasizes treatment response, including cbt, deep tms, and neural predictors across OCD and related conditions.

keywords: brain structural connectome of adhd children
medial prefrontal glutamate and default mode network
topics.title: ADHD Brain Connectivity and Symptom Dimensions
topics.description: This cluster focuses on ADHD in children and adolescents, emphasizing how symptom dimensions and subtypes relate to brain structure, structural connectivity, and functional characteristics. It also highlights network-level findings involving the default mode network, white matter microarchitecture, and glutamatergic measures.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
autism sensory subtypes predict task evoked insula connectivity
topics.title: Autism Developmental Brain and Subtype Variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and autism spectrum disorder across development, with emphasis on childhood and adolescence. It highlights atypical brain organization, functional connectivity, and clinically distinct subgroups or sensory subtypes.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Network Reorganization After Glioma Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glioma and brain tumor surgery alter brain networks and connectivity, with emphasis on functional remapping, structural disconnection, and network alteration. It also includes prediction of postoperative deficits and functional recovery, especially in relation to cognitive impairment and frontoparietal network connectivity.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
topics.title: Epilepsy Networks and Surgical Localization
topics.description: This cluster centers on network-level mechanisms and localization of epileptogenic regions in epilepsy, especially temporal lobe and focal epilepsy. It also includes dynamic connectivity and signal propagation features used to characterize epileptic brain activity and predict surgical outcome.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Stroke Lesion Networks and Recovery Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke and lesion-related structural and functional disconnection shape recovery across motor, language, and cognitive domains. It emphasizes prediction of outcomes using connectivity, reserve, and network-level markers.

topics.title: Brain Network Changes After Traumatic Injury
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how traumatic brain injury and related impacts alter structural and functional brain networks across different populations and injury types. It highlights multimodal neuroimaging evidence linking network disruption to symptoms, recovery, and cognitive outcomes.

keywords: sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Visual cortex mapping in glaucoma and blindness
topics.description: The cluster centers on structural and functional organization of visual cortex and its relationship to glaucoma, optic nerve degeneration, and retinal nerve fibre layer loss. It also includes retinotopic mapping and normative modeling approaches used to characterize visual system specialization and connectivity.

topics.title: Noninvasive Brain Stimulation and Network Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive brain stimulation approaches and their effects on cognition, symptoms, and neural network dynamics across conditions such as stroke and MCI. It also includes protocol factors and mechanistic measures like coil orientation, connectivity changes, and oscillatory markers.

topics.title: Neural Control of Food and Eating Behavior
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sensory, interoceptive, and cognitive control processes shape food-related decisions and eating behavior. It spans obesity and eating disorders, including binge eating disorder and anorexia nervosa, with interest in neural correlates and behavioral regulation.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal transitions and brain function in women
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual and menopausal hormonal changes relate to brain structure, connectivity, and cognitive function in women. It emphasizes midlife and perimenopausal changes, including hippocampal, emotion regulation, and memory-related effects.

topics.title: Exercise-Related Brain Plasticity in Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on age-related brain reorganization and white matter changes in older adults, especially within motor and callosal pathways. It also highlights how physical exercise and intervention relate to plasticity, functional gradients, and cognitive or mobility outcomes across the adult lifespan.

keywords: hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
topics.title: Sex Differences in Brain Development and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sex differences relate to brain structure, asymmetry, and functional connectivity across development and the lifespan. It also highlights cognitive effects and the hierarchical organization of these differences in youth.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
topics.title: Aging Effects on Brain Networks Across Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging and age differences relate to changes in brain structure, functional organization, and cognitive performance across adulthood and late adulthood. It emphasizes healthy cognitive aging, executive function, and lifespan changes in functional brain networks and connectomes.

keywords: brain age gap reduction following exercise mirrors clinical improvements
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Dementia Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain age estimation and prediction, especially accelerated brain aging as an early marker of neurodegenerative disease. It also includes work on dementia risk, Alzheimer disease progression, and the clinical interpretation and transportability of brain age models across populations.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Consciousness and Anesthesia Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster centers on how anesthesia alters consciousness, brain complexity, and thalamocortical network dynamics, especially under propofol and sevoflurane. It also includes EEG- and TMS/EEG-based measures used to assess anesthesia depth, propofol susceptibility, and related states such as delirium.

topics.title: Diabetes and Brain-Peripheral Vulnerability
topics.description: This cluster centers on how type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and cardiometabolic risk relate to brain function, structure, and vulnerability across organ systems. It highlights glycemic state, insulin action, and glymphatic/neurovascular mechanisms linking metabolic disturbance to neural outcomes.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Human Brain Metabolic Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on human brain metabolism and its network-level organization, with emphasis on glucose metabolism, cortical metabolic connectivity, and altered metabolic patterns in disease or task contexts. It also includes multimodal and high-resolution imaging approaches used to map metabolic signatures and gradients.

topics.title: Focused Ultrasound Thalamotomy in Tremor Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor and related movement disorders, with emphasis on motor improvement and durable outcomes. It also highlights connectivity-based and longitudinal network changes, especially within cerebellothalamic circuits, as markers of treatment response.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation in Parkinson’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation for Parkinson’s disease, especially subthalamic nucleus stimulation and related clinical response. It emphasizes connectomic, functional network, and predictive approaches aimed at personalizing DBS programming and outcomes.

topics.title: Developmental Organization of Functional Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain networks and cortical organization change across childhood, adolescence, and youth. It emphasizes developmental trajectories, reorganization, and individualized functional mapping of the connectome.

topics.title: Neonatal Brain Development in High-Risk Infants
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain structural and white matter development in neonates, especially those born preterm or with congenital heart disease. It emphasizes maturation, microstructural change, and developmental outcomes across early life conditions such as hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy and hypothermia.

keywords: maturation trajectories of functional brain network topology
topics.title: Early Brain Network Development in Preterm Infants
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neonatal and infant brain networks develop, with emphasis on functional connectivity, connectome gradients, and maturation of network topology. It includes studies of preterm birth, early developmental trajectories, and altered network organization in infancy and early childhood.

keywords: an automatic pipeline for sulcal feature extraction
benchmarke spatiotemporal fetal brain atlas construction
topics.title: Automated Fetal and Infant Brain Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and clinical MRI pipelines for fetal and infant brain mapping, including segmentation, surface extraction, and atlas construction. The abstracts emphasize developmental cortical folding, cerebellar surfaces, and template-based analysis across prenatal and early postnatal imaging.

topics.title: PTSD, Depression, and Suicide Risk Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on psychiatric risk states including PTSD, major depressive disorder, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts. It emphasizes brain network dysfunction and structural-functional alterations in amygdala and hippocampal measures.

topics.title: Human Cortical Cytoarchitecture and Atlas Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on cytoarchitectonic atlases and parcellation of the human cortex, especially references to the Julich and BigBrain resources. It emphasizes mapping cortical areas such as Broca region using cellular organization and surface-based geometry.

keywords: a quantitative comparative analysis of fiber bundles
topics.title: Diffusion MRI of White Matter Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI approaches for characterizing white matter microstructure, tract integrity, and developmental or age-related change. It also includes comparative tract analysis, normative modeling, and links to mesoscale or cellular-level histology.

keywords: spectral wise self supervise deep learning reconstruction
microstructure preserving diffusion mri super resolution
topics.title: Deep Learning Methods for Diffusion MRI Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and self-supervised methods for MRI reconstruction, denoising, and acceleration, especially in diffusion imaging. It also includes related work on super-resolution, registration, and efficient analysis pipelines for high-quality MRI processing.

keywords: ml models for multiple system atrophy classification use mri
topics.title: Explainable MRI-Based Neurodegenerative Diagnosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-driven classification and diagnosis of neurodegenerative and related neurological conditions, including dementia and multiple system atrophy. It emphasizes explainable, reliable, and robust deep learning approaches, often using pretrained models and multimodal interpretation.

topics.title: Generalizable Brain MRI Analysis Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on robust and generalizable approaches for brain MRI analysis, including anomaly detection, segmentation, and multimodal learning. It emphasizes domain adaptation, ensemble methods, and self-supervised or foundation-model-based strategies across stroke, tumor, and other neuroimaging tasks.

keywords: deep learning for automated segmentation of medial temporal lobe subregions
topics.title: Automated Longitudinal Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and atlas-based MRI segmentation methods, especially for hippocampal and medial temporal lobe structures. It also includes longitudinal stability, harmonization, and benchmark studies for robust morphometric neuroimaging.

keywords: chronic inflammation bridges brain clearance dysfunction
topics.title: Glymphatic, Myelin, and Iron Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on age-related and disease-related microstructural brain changes, especially glymphatic dysfunction, white matter alterations, and iron or myelin measures. It spans multiple sclerosis, Fabry disease, and other conditions assessed with diffusion and magnetic susceptibility imaging.

topics.title: Adolescent Depression and Early Life Stress
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early life stress, childhood trauma, and adversity relate to adolescent depression and broader psychopathology. It also emphasizes resilience and developmental risk factors in youth and adolescents.

keywords: brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
topics.title: Neural Predictors of Depression Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder, especially treatment resistant depression and response to interventions such as electroconvulsive therapy and stanford neuromodulation therapy. It emphasizes brain connectivity, network dynamics, and biomarker approaches for predicting antidepressant treatment response and clinical efficacy.

keywords: cortical microarchitecture along major vascular territories
post mortem human midbrain t2*w mri
topics.title: Cortical and Midbrain Vascular Mapping
topics.description: These studies focus on mapping and quantifying vascular structure across cortical territories and the human midbrain using high-resolution MRI and related analyses. They emphasize vessel morphology, venous anatomy, and microarchitectural relationships in post mortem and in vivo human tissue.

keywords: fast accurate population receptive field prf mapping
topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI Methods and Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-resolution fMRI approaches for mapping receptive fields, improving distortion correction, and supporting functional connectivity studies. It also includes challenges from high-field imaging such as sensitivity, inhomogeneity, and implant-related artifacts.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Social Cognition and Norms
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain systems support social cognition, empathy, perspective taking, and responses to fairness or norm violations. It also includes lesion-based and clinical evidence linking specific regions and disorders to social behavior and interpersonal traits.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Risk and Reward Decisions
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural systems support decision making under risk, exploration-exploitation tradeoffs, and reward learning. It highlights neural correlates, value computation, and prediction error signals across curiosity, risk taking, and reinforcement learning tasks.

keywords: choroid plexus volume mediates the link between insomnia severity and memory impairment
topics.title: Human Sleep Dynamics, Aging, and Glymphatic Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on sleep in humans, emphasizing age-related changes, brain-state and functional connectivity dynamics, and physiological mechanisms such as glymphatic and cerebrospinal fluid coupling. It also includes links between sleep disruption, locus coeruleus activity, and memory-related outcomes.

topics.title: White Matter Hyperintensity Pathology and Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensity burden, its spatial and spatiotemporal progression, and related microstructural changes across neurodegenerative and small vessel disease contexts. It also links vascular and fluid-transport processes, including blood-brain barrier water exchange and perivascular clearance, to pathological and clinical features.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
topics.title: Collaborative Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Workflows
topics.description: This cluster centers on infrastructure, standards, and workflow tools that support neuroimaging data management, sharing, and analysis. It also includes applications in collaborative clinical and aging-related neuroimaging research.

topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on cerebrovascular reactivity, baseline cerebral blood flow, and regional perfusion dynamics across brain tissue and hemispheres. The abstracts emphasize physiological responses to hypercapnia, hypoxia, and oxygen breathing, along with asymmetry and normative modeling of cerebrovascular function.

keywords: cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
topics.title: Brain-Cardiac Interactions in fMRI Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on interactions between cardiac, cardiorespiratory, and neural signals, especially how pulsatility and heart rate variability shape fMRI and brain dynamics. It also includes studies of autonomic failure, sleep, aging, and multimodal ECG-fMRI integration for analyzing physiological influences on brain activity.

keywords: a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Harmonization Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on harmonization approaches for reducing site and batch effects in neuroimaging and related multi-site data. It emphasizes standardized assessment, open-source tools, and model-based strategies for improving harmonisation efficacy across heterogeneous datasets.

keywords: discordance between spatial and population correlations from human brain imaging data
spatial trends in brain maps evidence and recommendation
topics.title: Spatial Statistics for Brain Map Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on statistical and computational methods for analyzing spatial patterns, correlations, and associations in brain maps and neuroimaging summary data. It emphasizes testing, projection, interpolation, and estimation approaches for comparing multimodal and population-level brain imaging signals.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fear and Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on fear conditioning, extinction, and anxiety-related neural responses, especially involving amygdala activity and autonomic nervous system changes. It also includes studies of human stress responses and multimodal imaging of threat processing.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation, cognitive reappraisal, and emotion processing are represented in the amygdala and related neural systems. It also includes clinical and affective features such as major depressive disorder, alexithymia, arousal, and altered neural responses.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Brain Connectivity in Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on transdiagnostic patterns linking brain structure and functional connectivity across psychiatric disorders and symptom dimensions. It emphasizes shared and disorder-specific cortical, connectome, and genetic associations relevant to mental health and bipolar disorder.

topics.title: Tau and Amyloid in Alzheimer Decline
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how tau and amyloid relate to cognitive decline and biomarker progression in Alzheimer disease and related dementia syndromes. It also emphasizes heterogeneity, resilience, and individual differences in tau-related neurodegeneration.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
development and evaluation of an intervention for adolescents
long term abacus base mental calculation learning
topics.title: Neural and Training Mechanisms in Dyscalculia
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental dyscalculia, math performance, and how interventions or training alter numerical processing. It highlights network reorganization, parietal numerical circuitry, and specialized neural pathways underlying arithmetic and learning.

topics.title: Auditory Neural Processing and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the auditory system encodes, learns, and remembers speech and non-speech sounds, including speaker identity, prosody, and semantic information. It also highlights individual differences, developmental change, and network-level organization in auditory and language-related cortex.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language is organized and processed in the brain, with emphasis on lateralization, speech and comprehension, and related neural systems. It also includes studies of aphasia, bilingualism, and structural pathways supporting language function.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Network Dynamics and Gait Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease research linking gait, freezing of gait, and cognitive or motor phenotypes to altered brain network organization. It also includes biomarker-oriented and imaging-based studies of connectivity, structural covariance, and multimodal network topology.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
topics.title: Resting-State Network Changes in Alzheimer Spectrum
topics.description: The cluster centers on resting-state brain and default mode network alterations across the Alzheimer disease continuum, including preclinical stages, MCI, and dementia. It also highlights cognitive dysfunction and network-level biomarkers linked to disease progression and vulnerability.

keywords: cerebellar thalamo cortical triple network alteration
topics.title: Psychosis-related brain network alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and the psychosis spectrum, emphasizing altered connectivity and network-level dysregulation across cortico-cerebellar, thalamo-cortical, and broader brain circuits. It also includes clinically relevant symptoms and trait markers such as auditory verbal hallucination and schizotypal features.

keywords: brain volume base predicted age deviation
normative modeling of reduced basal ganglia pvs
topics.title: Normative Brain Alterations in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and early psychosis, emphasizing normative modeling of brain morphology and age-related deviation. It also highlights basal ganglia perivascular space changes and white matter alterations assessed with fixel-based approaches.

keywords: hcp rs fmri pre processing pipeline
topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI denoising and reliability
topics.description: This cluster focuses on multi-echo fMRI processing methods for removing motion and other artifacts, often using ICA-based denoising and related preprocessing pipelines. It also emphasizes improving reliability and quality control in resting-state fMRI analyses.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
topics.title: Neural bases of visual mental imagery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how mental and visual imagery are represented in the brain, including sensory and cortical mechanisms, vividness, and individual differences such as aphantasia. It also links imagery to episodic simulation and autobiographical memory through studies of neural correlates and brain network organization.

keywords: cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
effect of epidural spinal stimulation on sensorimotor rhythms
topics.title: Motor Control, Observation, and Body Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how action observation, grasping, and motor execution are represented in primary motor cortex and related body representation networks. It also includes studies of cortical oscillations, adaptive updating, and epidural spinal stimulation in sensorimotor control.

topics.title: Neural Representation Alignment in Human fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on using deep learning and fMRI to study how visual and semantic information are represented in the human brain. It emphasizes representational alignment, encoding/decoding models, and brain-wide comparisons across natural scenes and neural hierarchies.

topics.title: Hippocampal Dynamics in Memory and Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and cortical dynamics supporting associative and episodic memory, including reactivation, encoding-retrieval balance, and retrieval failure. It also touches on network organization and control mechanisms shaping memory processing.

topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity and Brain Fingerprinting
topics.description: This cluster centers on individual differences in dynamic functional connectivity and connectome fingerprinting from fMRI data. It links brain state dynamics and connectivity patterns to behavior, reliability, and prediction.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: Aperiodic and Alpha EEG in Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on aperiodic and alpha-band EEG features, including spectral slopes and aperiodic exponent measures, in relation to cognitive states and task performance. It emphasizes how these neural signals vary across working memory and other cognitive contexts, often using MEG/EEG analytical approaches such as IRASA.

keywords: effective connectivity versus functional connectivity across visual cortical areas
topics.title: Effective Connectivity and Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on estimating and interpreting effective connectivity in human brain networks, often in relation to structural connectivity, brain state, and dynamic changes across resolutions. It emphasizes modeling approaches for comparing effective and functional connectivity and for addressing connectomics inverse problems.
